item.title: Iran War Diversifies Gulf Trade Routes in Ways That Might Prove Lasting
item.description: The forgotten trade that flows through Hormuz are containers bound for UAE’s huge port

item.title: Can Apple’s ‘Good Enough’ Siri Compete With ChatGPT?
item.description: Consumers have lots of AI choices so Apple’s assistant needs to match rivals

item.title: US-Iran Deal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz Bolsters Central Bank Doves
item.description: The US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz gives central banks more scope to hold rates.

item.title: Japan Ruling Party Still Mulling Sales Tax Cut to Zero for Food
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s ruling party is continuing to focus on whether to cut the sales tax on food to zero, largely casting aside proposals for a smaller reduction to 1% that would be easier for retailers to implement.

item.title: Anthropic Export Ban Deepens Fears About US Stranglehold on AI
item.description: For many politicians and technologists outside the US, their gravest fear about AI came true this weekend

item.title: Iran, Fed Take Market Spotlight Away From SpaceX Frenzy
item.description: Peace agreement spurs stock and bond rally, oil slump.

item.title: World Bank Plans $750 Million Delayed Loan for Kenya by End-June
item.description: The World Bank plans to disburse a long-awaited $750 million loan to Kenya by the end of the month partly to help the East African economy ward off external shocks.

item.title: UBS’s Baweja Says AI Stumble Key to Longer European Stock Rally
item.description: Europe needs to see several conditions in place, such as a broadening of capital expenditures away from artificial intelligence, before the region can sustain its equity rally, according to Bhanu Baweja, chief strategist at UBS Group AG.

item.title: Morgan Stanley’s Wilson Sees US Stock Market Rally Broadening
item.description: US stocks could get an additional boost from a rotation into cyclical, economically-sensitive industries that have lagged in the Iran war, according to Morgan Stanley strategists.

item.title: The Infuriating Rise of the $8 Ice Cream Cone
item.description: A combination of supply chain shocks and premiumization has turned America’s favorite summertime treat into a luxury good.

item.title: Goldman-Backed Energy Trader to Raise Debt After 96% Profit Drop
item.description: Danish energy trader InCommodities A/S plans to increase borrowing to support growth after profit slumped 96% last year amid subdued volatility in power and gas markets.

item.title: Birkenstock Readies First Bond Deal in Five Years for Buybacks
item.description: Birkenstock is lining up its first bond offering in more than five years as it seeks to replace existing debt and considers further share buybacks.

item.title: Patsnap Said to Confidentially File for HK, Singapore Dual IPO
item.description: Patsnap, an intellectual-property data provider, has filed confidentially for an initial public offering in Hong Kong and Singapore, snapping a prolonged drought for technology listings by Southeast Asian companies.

item.title: Xiaohongshu Is Said to Ready Hong Kong IPO Filing This Month
item.description: Xiaohongshu Technology Co., the Chinese creator of social platform RedNote, is preparing to confidentially file for an initial public offering in Hong Kong by the end of this month, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Shell Is Said to Select Aditya Birla for Sprng Energy Deal
item.description: Aditya Birla Group has emerged as the frontrunner to buy Shell Plc’s Indian renewable power producer, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Tencent-Backed Enflame Heads to IPO as China AI Chip Wave Grows
item.description: Tencent Holdings Ltd.-backed Shanghai Enflame Technology Co. has received approval for an initial public offering, a key step toward bringing to the market the last of the “four little dragons,” a group of leading artificial intelligence chipmakers.

item.title: Danantara Mulls 30-Year Bond Days After Fund’s Debut Sale
item.description: Indonesian sovereign wealth fund Danantara is exploring a sale of 30-year bonds following strong demand for its first ever global issuance.

item.title: Deutsche Bank Favors US Over Euro Corporate Bonds on Iran Impact
item.description: European corporate bonds are vulnerable to the aftershocks of the war in Iran, with spreads for both investment grade and junk bonds expected to widen by year end, according to strategists at Deutsche Bank AG.

item.title: India Trade Gap Shrinks, Sees Hurdles Ease With US-Iran Deal
item.description: India’s trade deficit in May narrowed marginally from the previous month, though it was wider than estimated, with officials saying an interim US-Iran agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz has improved the outlook.

item.title: Traders Cut South Africa Rate-Hike Bets on Iran Peace Deal
item.description: Traders have scaled back bets on additional South African interest-rate hikes after an interim US-Iran peace deal sent oil prices tumbling, potentially reducing inflationary pressures.

item.title: SpaceX Builds on Debut Rally With Further Gains After Record IPO
item.description: SpaceX shares jumped in premarket trading Monday, after its blockbuster debut Friday vaulted it into the ranks of the world’s most valuable public companies.

item.title: India, Indonesia Markets Enjoy Relief Rallies After Radical Interventions
item.description: It was arguably about buying time until a peace deal emerged. In the end they didn’t have to wait too long.

item.title: Carmen Li's Plan to Build a Futures Market for Compute
item.description: What it takes to build a GPU index.

item.title: Odd Lots: Li’s Plan to Build a Compute Futures Market (Podcast)
item.description: Odd Lots: Li’s Plan to Build a Compute Futures Market (Podcast)【原文：Odd Lots: Li’s Plan to Build a Compute Futures Market (Podcast)】

item.title: Hynix Billionaire Chey, Ex-Wife Fail to Solve Divorce Dispute
item.description: Billionaire Chey Tae-won of SK Hynix Inc. and his ex-wife Roh Soh-yeong failed to reach an agreement through court mediation, setting the stage for the resumption of their long-running divorce settlement battle.

item.title: Kazakhstan, Firebird Ink $10 Billion AI Deal With Nvidia Support
item.description: Kazakhstan signed accords with the startup Firebird Inc. on computing projects involving Nvidia Corp. that could bring as much as $10 billion in investment, as the Central Asian energy producer looks to position itself as an artificial intelligence hub.

item.title: UniCredit Asks BaFin to Review Commerzbank Statements Over Bid
item.description: UniCredit SpA asked Germany’s BaFin to investigate statements by Commerzbank AG that it says have undermined its takeover offer, further ratcheting up tensions between the two banks.

item.title: Kesko to Buy Dahl Scandinavia Operations in Its Largest Deal
item.description: Finnish retailer Kesko Oyj agreed to buy building materials distributor Dahl’s operations in Sweden, Norway and Denmark from Cie. de Saint-Gobain SA for €1.2 billion ($1.4 billion) excluding debt.

item.title: Lagarde Says Energy Price Hikes Spreading Through Economy
item.description: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde said high energy prices are starting to feed through to other parts of the economy, according to an interview with France Culture.

item.title: US-Iran Deal Poses Inflation Risk If Chinese Oil Demand Recovers
item.description: The US-Iran deal is setting the stage for a recovery in Chinese oil demand that risks driving up global inflation pressures, according to Bloomberg Economics, assuming the agreement holds and eventually restores energy flows to the world’s second-biggest economy.
